project.name: TUGAS-AKHIR-VAR-TATA-SURYA
project.currentFocus: Stabilize AR.js camera preview across mobile by normalizing #arjs-video layer placement, guarding scanner session races, and shrinking solar/planet model scales for phone markers.

lastAchievements: Found root cause in vendor AR.js: webcam preview uses #arjs-video appended to document.body with z-index -2, so scanner container background can hide live camera feed.
Added normalizeArVideoLayer + arjs-video-loaded listener to move/force-style AR video inside #arMount and preserve z-index stack on Android/iOS browsers.
Added scanner session guard (activeScannerSession) so close/reopen does not leave stale async boot writes that cause blank return-to-landing state.
Switched camera fallback from stream replacement to track applyConstraints flow to reduce AR.js source conflicts during front/back toggle.
Reduced marker model scales again for phones: solar system 0.03, fallback 0.12, and planet detail scales down to 0.022-0.032 range.
pendingIssues: Final visual calibration still requires real-device check on Infinix Note 40 and iPhone Safari.
If tap hit-zones drift after final marker scale, hitZonePosition/hitZoneRadius tuning is still required.
nextBestActions: Run full on-device checklist for AR start, switch camera, close scanner, and reopen scanner cycle.
After hit-zone tuning finalizes, set DEBUG_HIT_ZONES to false for production visual cleanliness.
Capture browser-specific notes for Android Chrome vs iOS Safari camera-switch limitations.
validationState: npm run preview startup + HTTP probe (127.0.0.1:4173): PASS (200)

designContinuity.currentConceptualAnchor: Handheld optical instrument panel (camera-first scanner HUD).
